payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

payload.message: 第三第四步放一起改吧
那我把第 3、4 步合并成一次收口，先给你准确算法，确认后我再改代码。核心是把“后台补时长”和“heartbeat 实时累计”统一成一套真实时间模型，避免你刚才举的 `20 + 100 + 60` 这种阈值错位。

content.text: 那我把第 3、4 步合并成一次收口，先给你准确算法，确认后我再改代码。核心是把“后台补时长”和“heartbeat 实时累计”统一成一套真实时间模型，避免你刚才举的 `20 + 100 + 60` 这种阈值错位。

payload.message:
合并后的改法我建议这样落：

1. 回退现在挂错的地方
- 去掉我加在 `n=13` 和 `n=15` 上的 `bgd`
- `appLaunch`、`enterForeground` 恢复成原来的事件形态

2. heartbeat 改成“时间差累计”，不再每秒盲加
- 现在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L302) 是每秒 `_appUsedTimeInSecond += 1`
- 要改成记录一个“上次累计时间点”，每次 tick 或状态切换时，用 `now - lastTickTime` 算真实经过的秒数
- 只有 app 在前台活跃时，这段差值才累计进前台运行时长
- 如果 vapp 在跑，同步累计进 `v`

3. 后台时长单独累计成一份 `pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`
- 热启动回来时：
  - 用 `foregroundNow - backgroundEnterAt` 算出后台时长
  - 不发 `n=15`
  - 这份时长直接累加到 `pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`
- 冷启动时：
  - 读取上次 `willTerminate` 存下来的后台时长
  - 也累加到 `pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`
- 这份值专门等下一个 `n=14` 发时再并进去

4. heartbeat 的触发条件也一起改
- 触发判断不能只看“前台累计时长”
- 要看：
  - `realAppAccumulatedForegroundDuration + pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`
- 这样你举的例子就会变成：
  - 前台 20
  - 后台 100
  - 回来后只再跑 60
  - 总计到 180，就立刻发 `n=14`

5. `n=14` 的 data 结构
- 我先按这个发：
  - `a = 前台真实累计 + 后台补时长`
  - `v = vapp 真实累计`
  - `bgd = 本次并入的后台补时长`
- 发完后清零：
  - `a/v` 的累计值
  - `pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`

这样处理后，你刚才那个例子最终会是：
- 上一个 `n=14` 发完后清零
- 前台 20 秒
- 后台 100 秒
- 回来再前台 60 秒
- 立刻发下一个 `n=14`
- 本次上报：
  - `a = 180`
  - `bgd = 100`
  - `v` 看当时 vapp 是否在跑

如果你认可，我下一步就按这个合并方案一起改。

content.text: 还有一个，如果前台100秒，后台挂起100秒，会怎么样

payload.message: 还有一个，如果前台100秒，后台挂起100秒，会怎么样

按我们刚刚这套“第 3、4 步合并后的正确算法”，如果心跳阈值还是 `180`，这个场景会这样：

场景：
- 上一个 `n=14` 刚发完，计数清零
- 前台 `100` 秒
- 后台挂起 `100` 秒

结果：
- 前台 `100` 秒会累计进前台真实时长
- 后台 `100` 秒不会靠定时器累加，而是在回前台时一次性算出，记进 `pendingBackgroundDurationForHeartbeat`
- 这时总量已经是 `100 + 100 = 200`，已经超过阈值 `180`

所以正确行为应该是：
- 一回到前台，就马上触发一次 `n=14`
- 这次上报里：
  - `a = 200`
  - `bgd = 100`
  - `v` 看这 100 秒前台里 vapp 是否在跑

也就是说，它不会再等 80 秒才发，而是“热启动回来后立刻补发 heartbeat”。

这里还有个实现细节我准备这样处理：
- 如果回前台时发现“累计总量已经 >= 阈值”，就在前台恢复流程里主动触发一次 heartbeat 检查
- 不能只等下一次 1 秒 tick，不然虽然只差 1 秒，但语义上还是晚了

如果你认可这个行为，我就按这个口径把第 3、4 步一起改。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

content.text: 再有一个前台60秒，后台10分钟，这时后台在第五分钟的时候被清掉了，应该怎么执行

payload.message: 再有一个前台60秒，后台10分钟，这时后台在第五分钟的时候被清掉了，应该怎么执行

这个场景把另一个关键点暴露出来了：不光要持久化“后台时长”，还要持久化“上一个 `n=14` 之后尚未上报的前台累计时长”。

按正确执行，应该这样：

场景：
- 上一个 `n=14` 刚发完，计数清零
- 前台 60 秒
- 进入后台
- 后台第 5 分钟被清掉
- 心跳阈值 180 秒

正确处理：
1. 进入后台那一刻
- 先把当前未上报的前台累计时长持久化下来
- 这里要存下 `pendingA = 60`
- 同时存 `backgroundEnterAt`

2. 如果后台第 5 分钟走到了 `willTerminate`
- 用 `terminateAt - backgroundEnterAt` 算出后台真实运行时长 `300`
- 把它累加到待 heartbeat 上报的数据里
- 这时持久化状态应该变成：
  - `pendingA = 360`
  - `pendingBGD = 300`

3. 下一次冷启动
- 恢复这两份持久化状态
- 因为 `pendingA = 360` 已经超过阈值 `180`
- 所以下一个 `n=14` 应该尽快发出，不需要再等
- 上报内容应该类似：
  - `a = 360`
  - `bgd = 300`
  - `v` 取决于那 60 秒前台里 vapp 是否在运行

也就是说，这个例子下不能只补 `300`，必须把前台那 `60` 也一起带上，不然总时长会少记。

还有一个必须说明的边界：
- 如果后台被系统直接杀掉，`willTerminate` 没回调
- 那我们只能可靠拿到前台那 `60`
- 后台这 `300` 没法精确知道
- 这时下一次冷启动应该：
  - 只恢复并上报 `pendingA = 60`
  - 丢弃上一次残留的 `backgroundEnterAt`
  - 不能用“冷启动时间 - 进后台时间”去倒推，否则会把 app 已经死掉后的时间也算进去

所以第 3、4 步合并后，状态至少要多一组：
- `pendingAppDuration`
- `pendingVappDuration`
- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
- `backgroundEnterAt`

如果你认可，我下一步就按这个版本改：
- 回退我之前挂在 `n=13/15` 的逻辑
- 把 `a/v` 的未上报累计也做持久化
- 把热启动/冷启动的后台补时统一并进 `n=14`。

- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

content.text: 还有，v的时间也要是准的，你准备怎么做

payload.message: 还有，v的时间也要是准的，你准备怎么做

要把 `v` 也做准，不能只补 `a`，必须把整套累计模型统一掉。我的方案会这样设计：

核心原则：
- `a` 表示 app 实际累计时长
- `v` 表示 vapp 实际累计时长
- 两者都不再依赖“每秒 +1”，而是依赖“状态切换时的时间差结算”

我准备这样做：

1. 统一做一个“结算器”
- 维护一个 `lastAccountingTime`
- 每次发生关键状态变化时，先用 `now - lastAccountingTime` 结算上一段时间
- 然后把 `lastAccountingTime` 更新到 `now`

2. 结算规则
- 如果这段时间 app 处于可计时状态，就把差值加到 `pendingA`
- 如果这段时间 vapp 处于运行状态，就把差值加到 `pendingV`
- 所以：
  - 前台普通停留：只加 `a`
  - 前台且 vapp 在跑：同时加 `a`、`v`
  - 后台且需要补真实时长时：
    - `a` 要不要加，看你定义 app 后台是否算运行时间。你前面已经明确要算，所以加
    - `v` 只有“进入后台那一刻 vapp 正在运行”时才加

3. 进入后台时要额外记录状态快照
- `backgroundEnterAt`
- `backgroundHadRunningVapp`
- 如有必要还记录 `backgroundRunningVappId`
- 这样热启动或 `willTerminate` 时，才能知道后台这段时间要不要补到 `v`

4. 热启动回前台时
- 算 `backgroundDelta = now - backgroundEnterAt`
- 这段时间：
  - 一定加到 `pendingA`
  - 如果 `backgroundHadRunningVapp == YES`，也加到 `pendingV`
- 然后清掉后台快照

5. 后台被清掉，走 `willTerminate` 时
- 同样算 `terminateAt - backgroundEnterAt`
- 持久化：
  - `pendingA`
  - `pendingV`
  - 以及必要时的 `pendingBackgroundDuration`
- 下次冷启动恢复后继续走 heartbeat

6. `n=14` 的触发条件
- 不能再只看原来的内存秒表
- 要改成看：
  - `pendingA >= heartbeatInterval`
  - 或 `pendingV >= heartbeatInterval`
- 这样你前面举的：
  - 前台 20 + 后台 100 + 回来再 60
  - 会在总量到 180 时立刻发，不会拖后

7. `n=14` 上报内容
- `a = pendingA`
- `v = pendingV`
- 如果你还要保留后台补偿明细，继续带 `bgd`
- 发完后清零：
  - `pendingA`
  - `pendingV`
  - `pendingBackgroundDuration`

你刚才这个问题对应的关键点是：
- `v` 是否要补后台时间，不是无脑补
- 要看“进入后台时 vapp 是否正在运行”
- 所以我会把这个状态一起持久化，不然 `v` 没法做准

还有一个边界我先说清楚：
- 如果 app 在后台被系统直接杀掉，且没进 `willTerminate`
- 那我们没法知道“挂起之后又过了多久才被杀”
- 所以 `a/v` 只能精确到“我们最后一个可观测生命周期点”
- 这个是 iOS 限制，不是代码策略能补齐的

如果你认可，我下一步就按这个统一模型把第 3、4 步一起改。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

- 不再把后台时长塞进 `n=13` / `n=15`

- 热启动和冷启动补出来的后台时长，统一并进 `n=14`
- `a` 会包含这段后台补时长，另外 `d` 里单独带 `bgd`
- `v` 也会按真实时间补，如果进入后台时 vapp 正在运行，就把这段后台时长也并到 `v`
- 旧逻辑我没有删，按你的要求都留成了注释

主要改在这几处：
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L295)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L487)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L732)
- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L217)

按你前面的场景，现在会是：

  - 回前台后不会立刻发
  - 再前台跑 `60s`，下一个 `n=14` 发 `a=180`，`bgd=100`

- 前台 `100s`，后台 `100s`
  - 一回前台就会触发 `n=14`
  - 发 `a=200`，`bgd=100`

- 前台 `60s`，后台 `10min`，第 `5min` 被清掉且进了 `willTerminate`
  - 下次冷启动恢复后，`startReporting` 会尽快补一个 `n=14`
  - 发 `a=360`，`bgd=300`
  - 如果当时 vapp 在跑，`v` 也会一起补

我还加了一个保护：
- 如果 `v` 在一次待上报周期里跨了多个不同的 vapp，代码会放弃携带单一 `i`，避免把 `v` 绑错 app；时长还会保留，只是不强绑 id。

本地没法做 `xcodebuild` 编译验证，因为当前环境的 `xcode-select` 指到的是 `CommandLineTools`，不是完整 Xcode。  
如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续帮你把这段逻辑再做一遍“边界场景清单”梳理，专门看有没有你们业务里的漏算点。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

content.text: 跟我讲一下整套逻辑吧

payload.message: 跟我讲一下整套逻辑吧

现在这套逻辑的核心目标只有一个：

不再靠“每秒 +1”去猜运行时长，而是用生命周期时间点去结算真实经过的时间，然后统一塞到 `n=14` heartbeat 里上报。

关键改动都在：
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L295)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L510)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L734)
- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L217)

**整体思路**
以前是：
- 定时器每秒跑一次
- `a` 每秒加 1
- 有 vapp 在跑时 `v` 每秒也加 1

这个方案在前台能勉强工作，但 app 一旦进后台被挂起，定时器就不跑了，所以后台那段时间会丢。

现在改成：
- 前台时长用“当前时间 - 上次结算时间”算
- 后台时长用“回前台时间/终止时间 - 进入后台时间”算
- 这些时长都先累计在本地
- 只有达到 heartbeat 阈值时，才统一打成一条 `n=14`

**现在记了哪些状态**
`WBEventManager` 现在会持久化几类状态：

- `pendingAppDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的 app 累计时长
- `pendingVappDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的 vapp 累计时长
- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的后台补偿时长
- `pendingVappId`
  - 这一轮待上报时长对应的 vapp id
- `backgroundEnterTime`
  - 最近一次进入后台的时间点
- `backgroundHadRunningVapp`
  - 进入后台那一刻是否有 vapp 在跑
- `backgroundVappId`
  - 进入后台时运行中的 vapp id

这些状态的读写入口在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L153)。

**前台怎么累计**
前台的定时结算在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L510) 和 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L357)。

逻辑是：

- `vappUsageRecord` 还是每秒跑一次
- 但它不再直接 `+1`
- 它会调用 `accumulateElapsedUsageAtTime:`
- 这个方法用 `now - lastUsageRecordTime` 算出真实经过的秒数
- 这段时间一定累加到 `a`
- 如果这段时间里当前有 vapp 在跑，也累加到 `v`

所以前台 20 秒，不是加 20 次 1，而是多次用时间差慢慢累出来。

**进入后台时做什么**
进入后台时，`WBAppManager` 会走 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L217)，然后调用 `WBEventManager onAppEnterBackground`，真正的结算在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L777) 附近。

这一步会做 3 件事：

- 先把“进入后台前最后一段前台时间”结算进 `a/v`
- 把当前累计值持久化，防止进程没了丢数据
- 记录一份后台快照
  - 后台进入时间
  - 当时是否有 vapp 在跑
  - 当时的 vapp id

这样后面不管是热启动回来，还是走 `willTerminate`，都能知道后台那段时间要不要补到 `a`、要不要补到 `v`。

**回前台时做什么**
回前台时，`WBAppManager` 会先调用 [WBEventManager syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.h#L24)，具体在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L759)。

这一步会：

- 读取 `backgroundEnterTime`
- 用 `now - backgroundEnterTime` 算出后台真实时长
- 把这段时间加到 `a`
- 同时把这段时间加到 `bgd`
- 如果进入后台时 `backgroundHadRunningVapp == YES`
  - 这段时间也加到 `v`
- 清掉后台快照
- 立刻判断一次是否达到 heartbeat 阈值

所以你前面说的：

- 前台 100 秒
- 后台 100 秒
- 阈值 180

现在会在“回前台的那一刻”就发现总量已经 200，于是立刻补发一个 `n=14`。

**终止时做什么**
终止时走 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L298)，再到 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L793)。

这一步会：

- 如果当前还带着后台快照
  - 说明 app 是在后台阶段结束的
  - 就用 `willTerminate` 时间减后台进入时间，把后台区间补算进来
- 如果没有后台快照
  - 就只补最后一段前台时间
- 然后把累计值持久化

这样下一次冷启动时，还能把这一轮没发完的 heartbeat 接着算。

**冷启动怎么恢复**
冷启动恢复逻辑在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L308)。

它会把上次没发掉的：

- `pendingAppDuration`
- `pendingVappDuration`
- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
- `pendingVappId`

全部读回来。

然后等 `startReporting` 被调用时，在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L734) 会立刻检查：

- 如果恢复出来的累计值已经达到阈值
- 就马上补发一个 `n=14`
- 不需要再等新的 180 秒

所以你前面说的：

- 前台 60 秒
- 后台 10 分钟
- 第 5 分钟被清掉
- 如果走到了 `willTerminate`

那下次冷启动后，这一轮累计会被恢复，`startReporting` 时就能尽快发出一条 `n=14`。

**`n=14` 现在怎么组包**
heartbeat 出包在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L527)。

现在 `n=14` 的 `d` 里有这些字段：

- `a`
  - app 的总累计时长
  - 已经包含后台补偿时长
- `v`
  - vapp 的总累计时长
  - 如果进入后台时 vapp 在跑，后台那段也会补进去
- `bgd`
  - 单独记录这次 heartbeat 里有多少时长是后台补进来的
- `i`
  - 这轮时长明确只属于同一个 vapp 时才带

也就是说现在会出现类似：

```json
{
  "n": 14,
  "d": {
    "a": 200,
    "v": 200,
    "bgd": 100,
    "i": 123
  }
}
```

**为什么 `i` 有时不带**
这个是为了保证 `v` 不归错 app。

逻辑在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L343)。

- 如果这一轮 heartbeat 里的 `v` 都来自同一个 vapp
  - 就带 `i`
- 如果这一轮里混进了多个不同 vapp
  - `pendingVappId` 会被置成冲突态
  - 最终这条 `n=14` 不带 `i`

这样做的目的，是宁可不带 id，也不要把累计出来的 `v` 错绑到某个 app 上。

**现在不会再发生什么**
现在不会再发生：

- 后台挂起期间 `a/v` 完全不增长
- 热启动回来后还得再等很久 heartbeat 才触发
- 冷启动后上一轮时长全部丢失
- `v` 后台时长明明该算却没算

**还有一个边界要明确**
有一个场景还是没法绝对准确：

- app 进入后台后
- 系统直接把进程杀掉
- 且没有走 `willTerminate`

这种情况下：
- 我们只能恢复“进入后台前已经结算好的那部分前台时长”
- 那段未闭合的后台区间会被丢弃
- 因为代码在冷启动恢复时会把残留的后台时间点清掉，见 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L316)

这是故意这么做的，因为：
- 冷启动时间减后台进入时间
- 会把“进程已经死掉后的时间”也算进去
- 那会更不准

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接用你前面那几个例子，再按“当前代码”的执行顺序逐条手推一遍。

content.text:
现在这套逻辑的核心目标只有一个：

不再靠“每秒 +1”去猜运行时长，而是用生命周期时间点去结算真实经过的时间，然后统一塞到 `n=14` heartbeat 里上报。

关键改动都在：
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L295)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L510)
- [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L734)
- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L217)

**整体思路**
以前是：
- 定时器每秒跑一次
- `a` 每秒加 1
- 有 vapp 在跑时 `v` 每秒也加 1

这个方案在前台能勉强工作，但 app 一旦进后台被挂起，定时器就不跑了，所以后台那段时间会丢。

现在改成：
- 前台时长用“当前时间 - 上次结算时间”算
- 后台时长用“回前台时间/终止时间 - 进入后台时间”算
- 这些时长都先累计在本地
- 只有达到 heartbeat 阈值时，才统一打成一条 `n=14`

**现在记了哪些状态**
`WBEventManager` 现在会持久化几类状态：

- `pendingAppDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的 app 累计时长
- `pendingVappDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的 vapp 累计时长
- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
  - 尚未随 `n=14` 发出去的后台补偿时长
- `pendingVappId`
  - 这一轮待上报时长对应的 vapp id
- `backgroundEnterTime`
  - 最近一次进入后台的时间点
- `backgroundHadRunningVapp`
  - 进入后台那一刻是否有 vapp 在跑
- `backgroundVappId`
  - 进入后台时运行中的 vapp id

这些状态的读写入口在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L153)。

**前台怎么累计**
前台的定时结算在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L510) 和 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L357)。

逻辑是：

- `vappUsageRecord` 还是每秒跑一次
- 但它不再直接 `+1`
- 它会调用 `accumulateElapsedUsageAtTime:`
- 这个方法用 `now - lastUsageRecordTime` 算出真实经过的秒数
- 这段时间一定累加到 `a`
- 如果这段时间里当前有 vapp 在跑，也累加到 `v`

所以前台 20 秒，不是加 20 次 1，而是多次用时间差慢慢累出来。

**进入后台时做什么**
进入后台时，`WBAppManager` 会走 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L217)，然后调用 `WBEventManager onAppEnterBackground`，真正的结算在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L777) 附近。

这一步会做 3 件事：

- 先把“进入后台前最后一段前台时间”结算进 `a/v`
- 把当前累计值持久化，防止进程没了丢数据
- 记录一份后台快照
  - 后台进入时间
  - 当时是否有 vapp 在跑
  - 当时的 vapp id

这样后面不管是热启动回来，还是走 `willTerminate`，都能知道后台那段时间要不要补到 `a`、要不要补到 `v`。

**回前台时做什么**
回前台时，`WBAppManager` 会先调用 [WBEventManager syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.h#L24)，具体在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L759)。

这一步会：

- 读取 `backgroundEnterTime`
- 用 `now - backgroundEnterTime` 算出后台真实时长
- 把这段时间加到 `a`
- 同时把这段时间加到 `bgd`
- 如果进入后台时 `backgroundHadRunningVapp == YES`
  - 这段时间也加到 `v`
- 清掉后台快照
- 立刻判断一次是否达到 heartbeat 阈值

所以你前面说的：

- 前台 100 秒
- 后台 100 秒
- 阈值 180

现在会在“回前台的那一刻”就发现总量已经 200，于是立刻补发一个 `n=14`。

**终止时做什么**
终止时走 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L298)，再到 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L793)。

这一步会：

- 如果当前还带着后台快照
  - 说明 app 是在后台阶段结束的
  - 就用 `willTerminate` 时间减后台进入时间，把后台区间补算进来
- 如果没有后台快照
  - 就只补最后一段前台时间
- 然后把累计值持久化

这样下一次冷启动时，还能把这一轮没发完的 heartbeat 接着算。

**冷启动怎么恢复**
冷启动恢复逻辑在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L308)。

它会把上次没发掉的：

- `pendingAppDuration`
- `pendingVappDuration`
- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
- `pendingVappId`

全部读回来。

然后等 `startReporting` 被调用时，在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L734) 会立刻检查：

- 如果恢复出来的累计值已经达到阈值
- 就马上补发一个 `n=14`
- 不需要再等新的 180 秒

所以你前面说的：

- 前台 60 秒
- 后台 10 分钟
- 第 5 分钟被清掉
- 如果走到了 `willTerminate`

那下次冷启动后，这一轮累计会被恢复，`startReporting` 时就能尽快发出一条 `n=14`。

**`n=14` 现在怎么组包**
heartbeat 出包在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L527)。

现在 `n=14` 的 `d` 里有这些字段：

- `a`
  - app 的总累计时长
  - 已经包含后台补偿时长
- `v`
  - vapp 的总累计时长
  - 如果进入后台时 vapp 在跑，后台那段也会补进去
- `bgd`
  - 单独记录这次 heartbeat 里有多少时长是后台补进来的
- `i`
  - 这轮时长明确只属于同一个 vapp 时才带

也就是说现在会出现类似：

```json
{
  "n": 14,
  "d": {
    "a": 200,
    "v": 200,
    "bgd": 100,
    "i": 123
  }
}
```

**为什么 `i` 有时不带**
这个是为了保证 `v` 不归错 app。

逻辑在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L343)。

- 如果这一轮 heartbeat 里的 `v` 都来自同一个 vapp
  - 就带 `i`
- 如果这一轮里混进了多个不同 vapp
  - `pendingVappId` 会被置成冲突态
  - 最终这条 `n=14` 不带 `i`

这样做的目的，是宁可不带 id，也不要把累计出来的 `v` 错绑到某个 app 上。

**现在不会再发生什么**
现在不会再发生：

- 后台挂起期间 `a/v` 完全不增长
- 热启动回来后还得再等很久 heartbeat 才触发
- 冷启动后上一轮时长全部丢失
- `v` 后台时长明明该算却没算

**还有一个边界要明确**
有一个场景还是没法绝对准确：

- app 进入后台后
- 系统直接把进程杀掉
- 且没有走 `willTerminate`

这种情况下：
- 我们只能恢复“进入后台前已经结算好的那部分前台时长”
- 那段未闭合的后台区间会被丢弃
- 因为代码在冷启动恢复时会把残留的后台时间点清掉，见 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L316)

这是故意这么做的，因为：
- 冷启动时间减后台进入时间
- 会把“进程已经死掉后的时间”也算进去
- 那会更不准

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接用你前面那几个例子，再按“当前代码”的执行顺序逐条手推一遍。

# Collaboration Mode: Default

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

content.text: pendingBackgroundDuration和pendingAppDuration不是一样的吗

payload.message: pendingBackgroundDuration和pendingAppDuration不是一样的吗

不是“一样”，而是“包含关系”。

现在这套里：

- `pendingAppDuration`
  - 表示这轮还没随 `n=14` 发出去的 `a`
  - 它是总量
  - 包含前台累计，也包含补进来的后台时长

- `pendingBackgroundDuration`
  - 表示这轮 `a` 里面，有多少是后台补进来的
  - 它是组成部分
  - 只是为了单独回填到 `bgd`

所以关系是：

```text
pendingAppDuration = 前台累计时长 + pendingBackgroundDuration
```

比如：

- 前台 60 秒
- 后台 100 秒

那就会变成：

- `pendingAppDuration = 160`
- `pendingBackgroundDuration = 100`

最后发 `n=14` 时：

- `a = 160`
- `bgd = 100`

所以它们不是重复存同一份值，而是：
- 一个给 `a` 总量
- 一个给 `bgd` 明细

如果服务端其实不需要 `bgd` 这个拆分字段，那 `pendingBackgroundDuration` 确实可以不要，直接只留 `pendingAppDuration` 就够了。  
但你前面说了“data 要同步到 `a` 里面”，我理解成：
- 既要进 `a`
- 又要保留这段后台时长的单独标记

所以我才把两份都留了。

如果你想，我可以下一步把名字改得更直白一点，比如：

- `pendingAppDuration` 保持不变
- `pendingBackgroundDuration` 改成 `pendingBackgroundDurationComponent`

这样更不容易误会。

payload.message:
你这个现象我看了，先说结论：

这条 `n=14` 的“计时值”大概率不是算成了 `09:41:46 -> 09:48:35`，而是已经在 `09:45:35` 那一刻“截断结算”了，只是没有立刻发出去，拖到 `09:48:35` 才跟着后续 `app/event` 发出来。

看你两条数据：

- 第一条 `n=14`
  - `t = 795951705.859905`
- 第二条 `n=14`
  - `t = 795951934.958595`
  - `a = 229`
  - `bgd = 184`

这两个 `t` 的差值是：

- `795951934.958595 - 795951705.859905 = 229.09869`

也就是约 `229` 秒。

这和你说的 `09:41:46 -> 09:45:35` 是一致的：
- `09:41:46 -> 09:45:35 = 229` 秒

所以这条 `n=14` 的 `a=229`，实际上是在表示：

- 从上一个 heartbeat 到你回前台那一刻，总共累计了 `229` 秒
- 其中 `184` 秒被识别成后台时长，所以：
- 前台部分约是 `229 - 184 = 45` 秒

也就是说，这条数据本身更像是在说：

- 这轮 heartbeat 在 `09:45:35` 已经该结算了
- 不是一直算到 `09:48:35`

所以你说“从 41 到 48 应该是 420 秒”，按现在这条 `n=14` 来看，不应该这么算。  
如果这轮 heartbeat 已经在 `09:45:35` 被截断了，那 `09:45:35 -> 09:48:35` 这 `180` 秒，应该属于“下一轮 heartbeat”的累计，不该继续并进这一条 `a=229` 里。

真正不对的点在这里：

- 按当前数值看，`n=14` 在 `09:45:35` 就应该成立
- 但你是在 `09:48:35` 才在 `app/event` 里看到它

所以问题更像是：

- “heartbeat 结算时机”基本对上了
- 但“结算完没有立刻发出去”，上报时机晚了

结合现在代码，最像的问题点是：

- `syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground` 里确实会在回前台时补算后台时间，并判断是否达到阈值
- 达到阈值后会生成 `n=14`
- 但这条 `n=14` 只是先进队列，没有强制立刻 flush
- 所以你看到它滞后出现在后面的 `app/event` 里

所以我建议下一步改法是：

1. 保留现在这套“真实时间差累计”的逻辑不动  
2. 专门补一个“前台恢复后如果已经达到 heartbeat 阈值，就立刻上报”的逻辑  
3. 不让这条 `n=14` 继续在普通队列里等下一次批量发送时机

如果你同意，我下一步就只改这一点：
- 让“热启动回前台触发出来的这条 `n=14`”立即 flush，而不是等后面的普通批量上报。

payload.message:
这条 `09:50:35` 收到的 `n=14`，从现在这套代码看，其实是“应该有”的。

关键不要看你“什么时候看到请求”，而要看事件里的 `t`。

你这三条的时间轴其实是这样的：

- 第一条：
  - `t=795951705.859905`
  - 对应上一轮 heartbeat 的切分点

- 第二条：
  - `t=795951934.958595`
  - 和第一条相差 `229.10s`
  - 也就是这条 heartbeat 逻辑上是切在 `09:45:35` 左右
  - `a=229, bgd=184`

- 第三条：
  - `t=795952115.344897`
  - 和第二条相差 `180.39s`
  - 也就是这条 heartbeat 逻辑上是切在 `09:48:35` 左右
  - `a=180`

所以现在实际发生的是：

1. `09:41:46 -> 09:45:35`
   这一段被算成了第二条 `n=14`
   `a=229, bgd=184`

2. `09:45:35 -> 09:48:35`
   这一段被算成了第三条 `n=14`
   `a=180`

也就是说，这两条在“逻辑时间”上是连续的，没有重叠，也没有多发。

真正不对的地方不是“09:50:35 为什么又来一条”，而是：

- 你看到第二条是在 `09:48:35`
- 但它的 `t` 明明是 `09:45:35`
- 说明这条 heartbeat 在 `09:45:35` 就已经生成了，只是你到 `09:48:35` 才看到它被送出来

所以现在暴露的问题其实是：

- heartbeat 的“生成时间”是对的
- 但 heartbeat 的“实际发送时间”滞后了

你前面第一次说“从 41 到 48 应该 420 秒”，也是同一个原因：
- 不是一直算到了 `09:48:35`
- 而是在 `09:45:35` 就已经切了一次
- 只是那条你晚看到了

顺便说一下 `a=229, bgd=184` 这组数本身也说明：
- 前台部分约是 `45s`
- 后台部分约是 `184s`

所以如果你主观记的是“前台大约 20 秒、后台大约 3 分钟”，那代码算的是更接近：
- 前台 `45s`
- 后台 `184s`

这里后台时长是按生命周期时间点算的，不是按体感。

结论就是两层：

- 计时切分逻辑目前看是通的
- 上报时机不够“立即”，导致你看请求时间会误判

如果你要的是：
- 回前台一旦补算后已经达到 heartbeat 阈值
- 就立刻把这条 `n=14` 发请求出去

那下一步应该改“flush 时机”，不是再改 `a/v/bgd` 的计数逻辑。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>

content.text: 改“flush 时机”

payload.message: 改“flush 时机”

我先不动代码，先把“flush 时机”这一步怎么改说清楚，你确认后我再改。

我准备只改发送时机，不动现在的 `a/v/bgd` 结算逻辑。

改动点：

1. 在 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m) 里加一个“立即 flush 事件队列”的内部方法  
   作用：
   - 不等普通 `reportInterval`
   - 直接触发一次 `doProcessEventList`

2. 调整 [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m#L759) 的 `syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground`
   逻辑改成：
   - 先补算后台时间
   - 如果补算后已经达到 heartbeat 阈值，就生成 `n=14`
   - 紧接着立刻 flush 队列，把这条 `n=14` 马上发出去
   - 不再让它等到后续批量上报

3. 保留现在普通 heartbeat 的老发送节奏
   - 定时器自然累计到阈值的 heartbeat，仍然走当前批量上报节奏
   - 只有“热启动回前台补出来并已达阈值”的这类 heartbeat，才强制立即 flush

4. 旧逻辑不删，继续按你的要求注释保留
   - 新增的方法和改动的方法都补中文注释

这样改完后，你刚才那个场景里：
- `09:45:35` 如果已经补算出 `a=229`
- 那条 `n=14` 会在 `09:45:35` 左右就直接发出去
- 不会拖到 `09:48:35` 才看到

如果这个方案没问题，我下一步就开始改。

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
</app-context>
